channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built an AI résumé tool that refuses to lie about your experience
item.description: Most AI résumé tools have the same flaw: they hallucinate. Ask them to tailor your résumé for a job requiring "Rust experience" and they'll happily invent a Rust project you never worked on. It...

item.title: Azure Entra ID User & Role Management — Step-by-Step Practical Guide With A Simple Excercise
item.description: Before going into the step by step practical guide on Creating User Via Azure Active Directory (Microsoft Entra ID) and Assigning Roles with RBAC,I will like to define key concepts to learn:...

item.title: Why I built Trinavo for the MENA merchants Western platforms ignore
item.description: A bootstrapped MENA founder's take on the gap between Shopify and Salla, and why "free to start" matters more in MENA than the discourse admits. It's 2026 and a merchant in Cairo, Amman, or Dubai...

item.title: The N+1 Query That Killed Our Database, And How I Fixed It
item.description: Everything Worked…But Not Well APIs are affected by the way data is retrieved from the database, and that's something that affected a recent teammate and me during development. In our regular...

item.title: Docstrings vs Markdown Docs: What Should Developers Actually Write?
item.description: Every developer hits this wall eventually. You know you should document your code. You've heard the lectures. But then you're staring at your project and wondering... do I write docstrings? A...

item.title: Add SVGIcons MCP to Claude Code and Find SVG Icons from Your Terminal
item.description: Finding the right SVG icon is one of those small tasks that constantly interrupts development. You are building a UI, need a clean search icon, then a settings icon, then maybe a GitHub logo. You...

item.title: 3 CLI Tools You Can Buy with Crypto — No KYC, No Subscriptions
item.description: 3 CLI Tools for Developers — Buy Once with Crypto, Use Forever If you live in the terminal, you know the pain. Repetitive commands, lost session history, messy .env files with secrets accidentally...

item.title: COSS Weekly: OpenClaw competitor NanoClaw Raises $12M, Dust Raises $40M, Sonar Acquires Gitar, and more
item.description: This week in COSS: NanoCo, the company behind the OpenClaw competitor NanoClaw, turned down a $20M buyout offer and raised a $12M seed round; Dust raised a $40M Series B for its multiplayer AI...

item.title: How to know if you actually need mobile proxies (without buying any)
item.description: Every scraping project I start, the same question comes up: do I actually need mobile proxies for this target, or will residential or datacenter do? Picking wrong on this is the most expensive...

item.title: What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work
item.description: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.

item.title: The best Memorial Day sales you can shop this weekend
item.description: Memorial Day is here, meaning the seasonal sales are in full swing. If your plans over the long weekend involve pool parties or barbecues, now is a great time to pick up a portable speaker or set...

item.title: The pope’s AI encyclical isn’t really about AI
item.description: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical uses AI as a lens to diagnose older problems: concentrated power, eroding democracy, and a tech elite that shapes the world to its own advantage.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for being ‘profoundly human’ in the age of AI
item.description: Pope Leo XIV warned of the risks of AI and unconstrained technological power in his first major papal document released on Monday. Magnifica Humanitas is the pope's manifesto on "safeguarding the...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in days: Apply before May 27
item.description: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. This is your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
item.description: Monday recap. Same mess, new week. A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies...

item.title: 5 days left: Save up to $410 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes before prices increase
item.description: Early Bird savings for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 in San Francisco end May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now to save up to $410 before prices increase.

item.title: Here are our favorite last-minute deals from REI’s giant Anniversary Sale
item.description: REI’s annual Anniversary Sale — the retailer’s biggest of the year — is still going strong, letting you save on all kinds of outdoor essentials. If you’ve got a camping trip coming up, now is a...

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations...

item.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks. According to QiAnXin XLab, the...

item.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
item.description: Deal also launched the first quantum foundry company, but is there a need for it?

item.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
item.description: Ask a cybersecurity pro about Network Detection and Response (NDR) and you might still hear "Noisy," "Too much data." But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll...

item.title: The AI Era Is Creating a Bug Hunting Arms Race
item.description: As attackers ramp up their AI exploit development, the search for software vulnerabilities is changing rapidly.

item.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and...

item.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
item.description: A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware. The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans...

item.title: Harness, Scaffold, and the AI Agent Terms Worth Getting Right
Everyone is navigating AI security in real time — even Google
item.description: We're in the transition period -- all of us.

item.title: On Trails is a wandering tale that blends hiking, science, and history
item.description: Hiking is one of life's great joys. Turning off the screens and stepping out into nature for an extended period of time, perhaps even several days, is rejuvenating. Unfortunately, as someone with...

item.title: I tried Amazon’s Bee wearable and am both intrigued and slightly creeped out
item.description: Like other AI wearables, Amazon's Bee offers an odd combination of convenience and privacy anxiety.

item.title: Apple’s latest MacBook Air is $200 off in both sizes for Memorial Day
item.description: If you’re looking for a laptop that’ll still perform well several years from now, Apple’s latest MacBook Air is hard to beat. Now, in particular, is a good time to pick one up, as you can...
